content.subject: Request for independent tensor-check audit of 3+1 stress-energy identity
content.email_template:
Dear Professor/Dr. [Name],

I am preparing a mathematical-relativity audit packet for a scalar-field stress-energy evolution identity in 3+1 form. I am not asking for endorsement of any collapse theorem. I am only asking whether the displayed tensor calculation is correct under the stated conventions.

The conventions are signature (-,+,+,+), future unit normal n^a n_a = -1, gamma_ab = g_ab + n_a n_b, and partial_t = N n + beta. The candidate identity to audit is:

(partial_t - Lie_beta) rho = N[-D_i j^i + K_ij S^ij + K rho - 2 a_i j^i], with a_i = D_i log N.

Could you review the attached short derivation for sign, lapse/shift, projection, and boundary-flux correctness? A brief written verdict identifying confirmed terms, corrections, and scope limitations would be sufficient.

This request does not ask you to validate WEC preservation, an energy estimate, a continuation criterion, finite-time collapse, or unrestricted gravity closure.

Sincerely,
Inacio F. Vasquez

boundary: admissible Route B preparation object only
